member.name: M:VL.IO.Raft.ClusterMemberExtensions.Split(DotNext.Net.Cluster.IClusterMember,System.Net.EndPoint@,System.Boolean@,System.Boolean@,DotNext.Net.Cluster.ClusterMemberStatus@)
member.summary: Splits a cluster member into its individual properties.
member.param: True if this member is the current leader.
True if this is a remote member; false if it is the local node.
Availability status of this member (Unknown, Available, or Unavailable).

member.name: T:VL.IO.Raft.LeaderElection

M:VL.IO.Raft.LeaderElection.Update(System.String@,System.Boolean@,System.Boolean@,System.Int64@,VL.Lib.Collections.Spread{DotNext.Net.Cluster.IClusterMember}@,System.String@,VL.Lib.Collections.Spread{System.String},System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Updates leader election state. Enable must be true for the cluster to start.
member.param: Host:port of the current leader, empty if no leader is known yet.
True if this machine is the current leader.
True if any leader is known.
Current Raft term number. Increments on every election.
All cluster members. Use Split (IClusterMember) to read per-member properties.
Error message if the cluster failed to start, otherwise empty.
IP addresses or hostnames of all machines in the cluster, in the same order on every machine.
TCP port all machines listen on.
Index of this machine in the Hosts list. When Prioritize is enabled, lower index means higher election priority (more likely to become leader).
When true, scales election timeouts by LocalId so the node with the lowest index is most likely to win. When false, all nodes have equal chance (standard Raft behaviour).
When true (default), Raft internal messages are written to the vvvv log. Errors written to the Error pin are unaffected.
Set to true to start the cluster.
